assumptions: Weights sum to 1.0; each sub-score in [0,1].
limitations: Heuristic blend; not a liquidity guarantee.

assumptions: Fixed transparent weights across OI/flow/Greek tilts.
limitations: Heuristic; not execution advice.

references.title: Bollen, N. P. B., & Whaley, R. E. (2004), Does net buying pressure affect the shape of implied volatility functions?, Journal of Finance, 59(2), 711-753
Pan, J., & Poteshman, A. M. (2006), The information in option volume for future stock prices, Review of Financial Studies, 19(3), 871-908

analyst_summary: For SPY, the aggregate options surface leans bullish — more call-heavy OI and flow. That reflects overall market positioning in the chain, not a specific actor; heavy clusters can sometimes line up with large books, but we do not label institutions. Model confidence is 60% for the next 1-2 weeks horizon.

scenarios.label: Bullish continuation
scenarios.narrative: Upside continuation if SPY holds above option-supported levels.

scenarios.label: Bearish unwind
scenarios.narrative: Downside pressure if hedging demand rises and calls unwind in SPY.

scenarios.label: Range-bound
scenarios.narrative: Mean-reverting regime likely if neither side gains flow dominance.

signals.name: Call Open Interest Share
signals.explanation: A larger share of call OI points to aggregate upside interest in the chain — sometimes consistent with sizable call interest, but not exclusive to any one player.

signals.name: Put/Call OI Ratio
signals.explanation: Elevated put/call OI can indicate hedging pressure or defensive positioning.

signals.name: Intraday Options Flow (Calls Share)
signals.explanation: Call-heavy volume relative to puts may indicate bullish tactical flow.

signals.name: Gamma Tilt
signals.explanation: Positive gamma tilt can support stabilization and upside continuation.

signals.name: Dollar Call Flow Share
signals.explanation: Dollar call volume ÷ total dollar volume — economic magnitude of flow, not just contract counts (Bollen & Whaley 2004; Pan & Poteshman 2006).

signals.name: Dollar Put/Call Open Interest
signals.explanation: Dollar-weighted put/call OI ratio — magnitude-weighted positioning vs simple counts (Bollen & Whaley 2004; Pan & Poteshman 2006).

assumptions: 100 multiplier per standard equity option contract.
limitations: Mids can be stale; wide spreads distort dollar notionals.

assumptions: Delta taken from vendor or BSM-enriched ``OptionGreeks``.
limitations: Net delta is a notional exposure snapshot, not a hedge recommendation.

signals.name: ATM Implied Volatility · IV Rank
signals.explanation: ATM IV is 18.00% (percentage points). Intra-chain IV rank 33% — how that ATM print ranks versus all implied volatilities in this snapshot.

signals.name: 25-Delta Skew
signals.explanation: IV of the ~25-delta put minus the ~25-delta call (nearest expiry). Positive skew often reflects downside protection demand. Wing richness (butterfly) and 10Δ skew extend the surface read (Carr & Wu 2009; Bates 2000).

signals.name: Term Structure
signals.explanation: Need two expirations with ATM IV to infer term structure.

assumptions: IV rank is empirical percentile within the current chain cross-section.
limitations: Single-snapshot rank is not a long-horizon implied-vol history.

assumptions: 25-delta IVs chosen by nearest delta match on the near slice.
limitations: Two-point term structure is coarse vs a full surface.

signals.name: Unusual Activity Score
signals.explanation: Counts strikes where volume is elevated versus open interest (ratio > 2, volume > 500), scaled into a 0–100-style intensity score.

signals.name: Aggregate Volume / OI
signals.explanation: Total options volume divided by total open interest — higher values can indicate more tactical trading versus stuck inventory.

signals.name: Dominant Flow Moneyness
signals.explanation: Delta-bucketed open interest and dollar volume — shows whether flow concentrates at the money or in wings.  Dominant call bucket atm; dominant put bucket atm (delta bands).

assumptions: High volume vs OI on the same line is flagged as unusual activity.
limitations: Does not separate opening vs closing; provider volume can be noisy.

assumptions: Uses ``greeks.theoretical_price`` when present.
limitations: Ignores borrow, discrete dividends, and microstructure beyond mid.

assumptions: Buckets use absolute delta from ``OptionGreeks`` when present.
limitations: Vendor delta may differ from model-implied delta.

signals.name: Net Gamma Exposure
signals.explanation: Sum of gamma × OI × 100 × spot for calls minus puts — a coarse dealer-gamma proxy.

signals.name: Gamma Regime
signals.explanation: Net gamma is positive — dealers are more likely to stabilize moves (fade extremes) if this positioning persists.

signals.name: Gamma Flip Strike (nearest expiry)
signals.explanation: Strike where cumulative per-strike GEX (calls − puts, OI-weighted, ×100×spot) crosses zero on the front expiry — a common “flip” narrative (SqueezeMetrics-style dealer gamma; Knuteson 2021, arXiv:2006.00975). No cumulative GEX sign change on the front expiry — flip strike unset.

signals.name: Net Delta Exposure (DEX)
signals.explanation: Sum of delta × open interest × 100 (calls + puts) — aggregate directional exposure in the chain (Lakonishok, Lee & Poteshman 2007; OCC/CBOE-style positioning summaries).

signals.name: Vanna Exposure
signals.explanation: Net vanna exposure — when negative, rising IV forces dealers to sell delta, amplifying downside moves. When positive, IV spikes trigger buying. Classified regime: neutral.

signals.name: Charm Exposure
signals.explanation: OI-weighted charm (∂Δ/∂T): positive net charm often means delta drifts up with time, so dealers may lean into selling at the open (overnight drift). Overnight delta drift: neutral.

assumptions: Nearest listed expiry only; OI-weighted gamma with spot scaling.
limitations: Dealer positioning is inferred heuristically from public chain data.

assumptions: Delta taken from vendor or BSM-enriched ``OptionGreeks``.
limitations: Net delta is a notional exposure snapshot, not a hedge recommendation.

assumptions: Call minus put vanna at nearest expiry; flip via cumulative strike ladder.
limitations: Public chain vanna is model-dependent (BSM here).

assumptions: Calendar-time charm from BSM when ``greeks.charm`` is populated.
limitations: Overnight drift label is a coarse heuristic vs path-dependent reality.

signals.name: Max Pain Strike
signals.explanation: Strike that minimizes total intrinsic value paid to option holders at expiration (nearest expiry only) — a common magnet narrative, not a forecast.

signals.name: Strike Magnets (OI)
signals.explanation: Largest open-interest strikes in the nearest expiration — where inventory tends to cluster.

signals.name: Implied Move (Straddle)
signals.explanation: ATM straddle mid / spot for the nearest expiration — a rough implied move proxy; when DTE is available we also show Brenner & Subrahmanyam (1988) σ from the straddle and DTE-consistent daily/period moves. DTE=7, σ_BS88≈3.19% ann.; daily≈0.2012%, horizon≈0.5323% (see implied_move_detail).

assumptions: Max pain minimizes total intrinsic value weighted by OI at one expiry.
limitations: Max pain is a static snapshot heuristic.

assumptions: European-style straddle on matched ATM call/put mids.
limitations: Ignores skew/smile beyond the chosen ATM pair.

assumptions: Uses ``greeks.itm_probability`` when populated.
limitations: Exercise dynamics and borrow are not modeled.

regime_explanation: Net gamma is positive — dealers are more likely to stabilize moves (fade extremes) if this positioning persists.

assumptions: IV rank is empirical percentile within the current chain cross-section.
limitations: Single-snapshot rank is not a long-horizon implied-vol history.

assumptions: 25-delta IVs chosen by nearest delta match on the near slice.
limitations: Two-point term structure is coarse vs a full surface.

assumptions: Delta taken from vendor or BSM-enriched ``OptionGreeks``.
limitations: Net delta is a notional exposure snapshot, not a hedge recommendation.

assumptions: European-style straddle on matched ATM call/put mids.
limitations: Ignores skew/smile beyond the chosen ATM pair.

assumptions: Call minus put vanna at nearest expiry; flip via cumulative strike ladder.
limitations: Public chain vanna is model-dependent (BSM here).

assumptions: Calendar-time charm from BSM when ``greeks.charm`` is populated.
limitations: Overnight drift label is a coarse heuristic vs path-dependent reality.

assumptions: Buckets use absolute delta from ``OptionGreeks`` when present.
limitations: Vendor delta may differ from model-implied delta.
